Title: GmMYB4 Positively Regulates Isoflavone Biosynthesis via the GmMAPK6-GmMYB4-MBW Module in Soybean

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive compounds beneficial for human health
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms the isoflavone branch and promotes target-product accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes aryl migration/oxygenation of flavanones to form 2-hydroxyisoflavanone intermediates in isoflavone biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control point for isoflavone accumulation in this study
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increases total isoflavone content in soybean leaves and seeds
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression in stable soybean lines; metabolite profiling of isoflavone content; RT-qPCR
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in GmMYB4 overexpression lines and downregulated in gmmyb4 mutants; upregulated in GmMAPK6 overexpression hairy roots
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Endoplasmic reticulum (inferred for cytochrome P450 isoflavone synthase)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful enzyme target for boosting isoflavone accumulation, especially combined with upstream regulator GmMYB4/GmMAPK6 manipulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmIFS2 expression is positively associated with GmMYB4 activity. 2. GmIFS2 overexpression significantly increased total isoflavone content in R2 leaves and R8 seeds. 3. GmIFS2 is a downstream metabolic determinant in the GmMAPK6-GmMYB4-MBW module.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The MBW complex represses GmIFS2 promoter activity, while GmMYB4 alleviates this repression indirectly by disrupting MBW complex formation.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Natural growth and controlled growth chamber conditions as described; soybean hairy root transformation system

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: R2R3-MYB family; bHLH TF-binding motif in R3 domain; EAR repression motif
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in roots, stems, leaves, flowers, pods and seeds, with highest expression in flowers
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed broadly; manipulated in hairy roots, leaves, and seeds; overexpression lines show increased transcript abundance, mutants/RNAi show reduced abundance
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive compounds beneficial for human health
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes isoflavone accumulation while suppressing anthocyanin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Transcriptional regulation and metabolic branch selection between isoflavone and anthocyanin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key positive regulator identified by GWAS and functional validation; central switch balancing competing isoflavone versus anthocyanin branches
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: GmIFS2; GmANS3; GmPAL1.3; GmC4H; GmCHS8; GmCHR1; GmCHI1; GmIFS1
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates isoflavone-pathway gene expression indirectly and suppresses anthocyanin-pathway gene GmANS3 indirectly by disrupting MBW complex assembly
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Indirect transcriptional regulation via interaction with bHLH partners and interference with MBW complex formation
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Acts through MYB-bHLH-WD40 (MBW) regulatory context; interacts with GmTT8, GmGL3, and GmEGL3; disrupts GmMYB115-GmTT8 complex formation
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: GmTT8; GmGL3; GmEGL3; GmMAPK6; GmMAPKK2; GmMYB115
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS; haplotype analysis; hairy root overexpression and RNAi; stable overexpression and CRISPR knockout; RNA-seq; DAP-seq; EMSA; Y2H; Y1H; BiFC; LCI; Dual-LUC; metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S constitutive promoter; native GmIFS2pro and GmANS3pro in reporter assays
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Three field environments in Nanjing for GWAS (2014, 2015, 2018); controlled growth chamber conditions for transgenic plants
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Decreases anthocyanin content in leaves while increasing isoflavones; alters flavonoid/proanthocyanidin partitioning
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Favourable haplotype GmMYB4H1 and overexpression/editing strategies could be used to enhance soybean isoflavone content; should consider effects on anthocyanin branch
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GWAS and haplotype analysis identified GmMYB4 as the candidate causal gene at qGlc11 for glycitein-class content. 2. Overexpression increased TIC, GEC, DAC and GLC, whereas RNAi/knockout reduced them in hairy roots, leaves and seeds. 3. GmMYB4 interacts with bHLH proteins to disrupt the GmMYB115-GmTT8-containing MBW complex, relieving repression of GmIFS2 and reducing activation of GmANS3. 4. GmMYB4 therefore redirects flux toward isoflavones and away from anthocyanins.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: DAP-seq identified a preferred binding motif (CACCTAAC), but Y1H showed GmMYB4 did not directly bind promoters of 10 tested pathway genes, supporting an indirect mechanism. Double knockout with the homolog Glyma.12g032200 caused stronger reductions, indicating functional compensation.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: GWAS using resequencing SNPs from 219 accessions; RNA-seq (Illumina NovaSeq 6000); DAP-seq

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Overexpressed in soybean hairy roots for functional assays
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive compounds beneficial for human health
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes isoflavone accumulation by upregulating GmMYB4 and isoflavone biosynthetic genes and by phosphorylating GmMYB4
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Signal transduction and post-translational/transcriptional control of isoflavone biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Upstream signaling regulator linking MAPK cascade to isoflavone accumulation through direct action on GmMYB4
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: GmMYB4; GmPAL1.3; Gm4CL; GmCHI1; GmCHR1; GmIFS2; GmIFS1; GmANS3
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Upregulates GmMYB4 and multiple isoflavone biosynthetic genes; downregulates GmANS3
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Phosphorylation of GmMYB4 at Ser39 and positive transcriptional regulation of GmMYB4/pathway gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: MAPK cascade context; candidate interaction with GmMAPKK2
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Physically interacts with GmMYB4; no named stable complex established beyond MAPK signaling interaction
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Hairy root overexpression; BiFC; Co-IP; Y2H with constitutively active mutant; in vitro kinase assay; LC-MS/MS phosphosite identification; RT-qPCR; metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Constitutive overexpression vector in hairy roots
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May suppress anthocyanin branch via GmANS3 downregulation; broader MAPK pleiotropy not resolved
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising upstream engineering target for increasing soybean isoflavone content through combined transcriptional and post-translational activation of GmMYB4
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmMAPK6 physically interacts with GmMYB4. 2. Constitutively active GmMAPK6 phosphorylates GmMYB4 at Ser39 in vitro. 3. Overexpression of GmMAPK6 increases isoflavone content and induces GmMYB4 plus several isoflavone-pathway genes while repressing GmANS3. 4. GmMAPK6 is an upstream positive regulator in the GmMAPK6-GmMYB4-MBW module.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper proposes both transcriptional activation of GmMYB4 and post-translational regulation via phosphorylation, but in vivo phosphosite validation remains future work.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: PPI prediction using STRING; LC-MS/MS phosphoproteomics for in vitro kinase reaction
